title: Media Page, Imports, and Bins

video.note: Covers the Media page, media storage, Media Pool, importing clips, changing frame-rate prompts, bins, folder structure, list and thumbnail views, metadata, keywords, and smart-bin awareness.

learningTarget: I can import footage into the Media Pool and organize it with bins so my edit can be built from clean source material.

bellRinger.prompt: When you have a folder full of random clips, what information helps you find the right shot quickly?

vocabulary.definition: The Resolve workspace focused on finding, previewing, importing, and organizing media.
The panel used to browse files on the computer or connected drives.
The project area that contains clips, audio, timelines, and bins available to the edit.
A folder inside the Resolve project used to organize media.
Information about a clip, such as shot, scene, take, comments, or technical details.
A bin that gathers clips automatically based on rules or metadata filters.

slides.title: Lesson 02 - Media Page, Imports, and Bins

assignment.title: Organized Media Pool
evidenceRequired: Google Drive link to a screenshot of the Media Pool with organized bins.
Google Drive or Docs link showing at least two metadata or note entries.
Short reflection explaining one organization choice that will speed up editing.

exitTicket: What is the difference between media sitting on the computer and media imported into the Resolve Media Pool?
